Our Story

A Slice of
Island Paradise

Sanzter Campsite sits on the beautiful shores of Cagbalete Island — the "Boracay of the Pacific" — just a short boat ride from Mauban, Quezon. We offer a unique experience that blends the warmth of farm life with the magic of island living.

Whether you're a family looking for a wholesome getaway, a group of friends seeking adventure, or a couple wanting to reconnect with nature — Sanzter Campsite is your home away from home on Cagbalete Island.

Family-Owned Farm and Campsite & Operated Since March 2023
Where You'll Sleep

Choose Your Adventure

From cozy tent camping to comfortable cottages — we have the perfect spot for every type of traveler.

Tent Camping

Bring your own tent and pitch it at our dedicated camping area, or rent one of ours for a hassle-free overnight stay. Just a 3-minute walk to the beach — fall asleep under a sky full of stars.

₱200 – ₱300 / head overnight
Tent Rental Available Own Tent Welcome Designated Pitch Area

Open Cottage

Our open-air native cottages let you breathe in the fresh farm air while enjoying a relaxed, shaded retreat. Perfect for groups who want to gather, dine, and unwind together in true island style.

₱1,000 / overnight
Open Air Farm View Group-Friendly

Kubo Accommodation

Experience the charm of traditional Filipino kubo living. Choose from small, medium, or big kubo units nestled within our grounds — the perfect blend of rustic comfort and island serenity. The Big Kubo includes free use of kitchen wares, cooking wares, and a gas stove.

Small Kubo — ₱1,500–₱2,000 Medium Kubo — ₱2,500–₱3,000 Big Kubo — ₱4,000–₱4,500
🍳 Big Kubo includes: kitchen wares, cooking wares & gas stove
Things To Do

Fill Your Days
with Wonder

Cagbalete Island is bursting with adventures — from exploring sandbars that appear at low tide to snorkeling vibrant coral reefs. At Sanzter Campsite, your fun starts at the doorstep.

⛵

Island Hopping

Visit four stunning stops: Yang-in Sand Bar, San Jose River, the lush Mangroves, and world-class snorkeling spots — all by bangka boat

🤿

Snorkeling

Dive into crystal-clear waters teeming with vibrant corals and tropical marine life at our designated snorkeling stop on the island hopping tour

🌅

Sandbar Walk

During low tide, walk the dramatic Yang-in Sand Bar stretching hundreds of meters — one of Cagbalete's most iconic sights

🌿

Farm Tour

Explore our working farm, see fresh produce up close, and connect with the authentic rhythm of island agriculture

🛶

Crystal Kayak

Glide over the clear shallow waters in a see-through crystal kayak and watch the ocean floor beneath you — an unforgettable experience. Starts at ₱150

🍖

BBQ Night

Grill fresh seafood under a canopy of stars at our campsite grounds — great food, great company, and the cool island breeze

Getting Here

Your Journey Starts Here

Cagbalete Island is just a few hours from Manila. Here's how to reach us at Sanzter Campsite.

🚌
1

Manila to Mauban

Ride a JAC Liner or Genesis Bus from Buendia/Cubao (Kamias) going to Mauban, Quezon. Travel time: 4–5 hours. Fare: around ₱400.00 each.

›
🏪
2

Mauban Port

Head to the port near the Mauban public market via tricycle ride (Fare: ₱20.00). Pay the ₱80 environmental fee per person at the tourism office located at the Mauban Port area before boarding.

›
⛵
3

Boat to Cagbalete

Take a passenger ferry (₱100/head) or charter a private boat (price varies depending on the number of guests — arrange at the tourism office). Ride takes 45–60 min.

›
🏡
4

Sanzter Campsite!

Arriving at the island, habal-habal riders (₱70/head) will take you straight to Sanzter Campsite. Welcome! Note: Fares may change with prior notice.

Pricing

Simple & Transparent Rates

No hidden fees. Straightforward pricing so you can plan your island escape with confidence.

Accommodation / Service Details Rate
Tent Pitching (bring your own tent) Per head, overnight ₱200 – ₱300
Tent Rental (at tent pitching area) Per head, overnight ₱500
Open Cottage Overnight use ₱1,000
Kubo (Small) Overnight stay ₱1,500 – ₱2,000
Kubo (Medium) Overnight stay ₱2,500 – ₱3,000
Kubo (Big) Overnight stay — includes kitchen wares, cooking wares & gas stove ₱4,000 – ₱4,500
Crystal Kayak Per person Starts at ₱150
💡 Good to Know: Rates may vary during peak season (Holy Week, summer, long weekends). Electricity is generator-powered (limited hours). Please bring toiletries, towels, and insect repellant. For the latest rates and availability, message us on Facebook or reach us through the contact details below.
